mechanism_narrative: A declining federal funds rate trend, as the Fed cut from 5.25% to 4.75% in September 2007, lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces borrowing costs, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month as markets price in easier financial conditions.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut to 4.94% in September 2007 vintage
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts below a critical threshold signals deteriorating residential construction demand that historically precedes broader consumption and credit contraction, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter as recession risk repricing occurs.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 25% from its 12-month prior level
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1330-1331 thousand units in August 2007, down sharply from 2005-2006 peaks near 2000
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following HOUST threshold breaches are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth combined with a rising unemployment rate signals a labor market inflection point that leads equity markets to reprice earnings expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month; neither series alone carries the same signal because payroll noise and unemployment lag differ.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth turns negative while UNRATE rises by 0.2pp or more over the same period
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138,099-138,265 thousand and UNRATE steady at 4.7% in September 2007
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS deceleration and UNRATE rise events are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Continued positive month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects healthy manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate output, supporting near-term investor confidence and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 114.4254 for August 2007 showing continued expansion
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO month-over-month readings are not associated with statistically significant positive 5-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where retail sales growth remains positive but housing starts are already contracting sharply, the divergence signals that consumer spending is being sustained by unsustainable credit conditions rather than fundamentals, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as the housing-led slowdown eventually spreads to consumption.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380,230 million in September 2007 vintage while HOUST has fallen sharply from 2005 peaks
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns during this RSAFS-positive/HOUST-declining regime are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window restricted to this regime.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises above a threshold that constrains the Fed's ability to continue easing, markets reprice the probability of future rate cuts downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as the monetary put is perceived to weaken.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3.0% while FEDFUNDS has been cut in the prior two months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 207.667-207.738 in August 2007, FEDFUNDS at 4.94% after September cut
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle under inflation constraint
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following CPI threshold breaches during easing cycles are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts continue to decline sharply despite the Fed actively cutting the federal funds rate, this divergence signals that monetary easing is failing to stabilize credit-sensitive sectors, indicating deeper financial stress that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; the signal requires both series moving in the diverging pattern simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 10% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS is simultaneously being cut
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS cut to 4.94% in September 2007 while HOUST fell to 1330-1331 thousand units, well below prior year levels
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle failing to stabilize housing
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where HOUST declines despite FEDFUNDS cuts, or the interaction model shows no incremental Rank IC over single-series HOUST or FEDFUNDS models.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where unemployment sits near cyclical lows, a slowdown in payroll growth signals the labor market has peaked and further deterioration is likely, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter as investors anticipate margin compression from wage pressure combined with slowing hiring.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% (near cyclical low) with PAYEMS growth slowing in September 2007 vintage
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during PAYEMS deceleration in low-unemployment regimes are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window restricted to this regime.
